result.markdown:
--- title: "Response bias modelling" format: html editor: visual html: toc: true toc-location: right toc-expand: true code-fold: true mathjax: true project: type: website output-dir: docs execute: freeze: auto --- ## Mixture modelling on core trials *Core* trials made up the majority (90%; 432/480 trials) of the task. They're what we've discussed in meetings so far: participants saw an equal number of trials from each cell of our 2 x 2 design (2 reward levels: low, high; 2 coherence levels: 20%, 40%). Writeup to come. Note: accidentally didn't exclude guessers, but in a way this is good as model still fits well (would just assign them a high guessing probability). ### Does the model fit? First, let's visually inspect how the model is fitting relative to the observed data. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: Overall, model fit is looking much better than for the simpler non-mixture version (circular response distribution with dispersion. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} y_rep <- posterior_predict( fit_combo_dist, newdata = combo_filt_clean, ndraws = 10 ) # 4. Get the observed values from the clean dataset y_obs <- combo_filt_clean$valuebias_rad # 5. Plot ppc_dens_overlay_grouped( y = y_obs * 180 / pi, yrep = y_rep * 180 / pi, group = combo_filt_clean$condition ) + theme_minimal() + labs( title = "Posterior Predictive Check by Condition", subtitle = "Comparing observed data to model simulations (Valid Participants Only)", x = "Value Bias (degrees)", y = "Density" ) + facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ### Is a response bias present? First let's see how the model has estimated *mu* (mean) in each reward x coherence condition. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Task-Directed Bias (mu1) by Condition (Degrees)
Reward Coherence Median Bias 95% Lower 95% Upper P-Direction
hi 0.2 7.05 3.94 10.24 1.000
hi 0.4 2.42 0.03 4.88 0.976
lo 0.2 3.29 0.06 6.59 0.978
lo 0.4 2.60 0.29 5.03 0.985
````` ::: ::: Takeaways: - When coherence is *low* (20%), there is a credible response bias for high-value trials but not low-value ones - When coherence is *medium* (40%), there is a credible (but small) response bias for both high and low-value trials. - When coherence is *high* (60%), there is no longer a response bias for high-value trials. Conversely, the response bias for low-value trials persists. ### How does bias change with coherence and reward? The nice thing about this modelling approach is that we can see the posterior distributions for *differences* in response bias across levels of coherence and reward (i.e., the range of plausible values for how much the response has actually shifted). In these graphs, the plotted point is the median difference and the whiskers are the 66% (thick) and 95% (thin) credible intervals. #### Effect of coherence Firstly, let's take a look at how coherence affects response bias within each level of reward. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` High Reward Low Reward 4000 4000 ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Mixture Model: Change in task-directed bias (mu1) across coherence
Condition Contrast Median Diff Lower 95% Upper 95% P-Direction
hi coherence0.2 - hi coherence0.4 4.586 2.391 6.992 1.00
lo coherence0.2 - lo coherence0.4 0.654 -1.598 3.113 0.71
````` ::: ::: Takeaways: #### Effect of reward ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Mixture Model: Reward-Induced Bias Shift (mu1)
Values represent High Reward minus Low Reward in degrees.
Coherence Level Median Diff 95% Lower 95% Upper P-Direction
Low Coherence (0.2) 3.73 0.58 6.96 0.99
High Coherence (0.4) -0.19 -0.87 0.49 0.70
````` ::: ::: Takeaways: - ### Does response precision change across trial types? Next, let's see how the model has estimated *kappa* (response precision) in each reward x coherence condition. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Task-directed precision (kappa1) by condition
95% Credible Interval
Coherence Reward Median Kappa 95% Lower 95% Upper
0.2 lo 0.150 0.082 0.275
hi 0.162 0.088 0.301
0.4 lo 1.955 1.067 3.565
hi 1.674 0.918 3.055
````` ::: ::: I won't do the full set of pairwise contrasts here as we don't care about most of them. As expected, there's a clear effect of coherence upon kappa (high-coherence trials produce a less dispersed distribution of responses). Note also that the model has found it harder to estimate precision for the catch (60% coherence) trials. This may be due to the lower amount of data available, but looking at the posteriors for 40% coherence trials, it could also be due to identifiability issues when estimating *kappa* for tighter distributions (i.e., once a distribution is already 'spiked,' even massive increases in *kappa* result in almost no visible change to the response spread). However, a key question remains: within each level of coherence, is responding less precise for low-reward trials? In other words, are people making less of an effort when they aren't going to win big? ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
coherence median_diff lower_95 upper_95 prob_hi_better
0.2 0.011 -0.01 0.042 0.871
0.4 -0.271 -0.68 -0.043 0.010
````` ::: ::: So yes: while there's no difference in response precision for low-coherence trials, it does appear that people are trying less hard for low-reward trials when coherence is higher. ### Guessing proportion Do people guess less on easier trials? ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} cell_guess_mix <- emmeans(fit_mixture_test, ~ trialval * coherence, dpar = "theta2") %>% gather_emmeans_draws() %>% mutate(prob_guess = plogis(.value)) # Transform to 0-1 probability scale # 2. Summary Table zero_test_guess_summary <- cell_guess_mix %>% group_by(trialval, coherence) %>% summarize( median_prob = median(prob_guess), lower_95 = quantile(prob_guess, 0.025), upper_95 = quantile(prob_guess, 0.975), pd = as.numeric(bayestestR::p_direction(prob_guess)), .groups = "drop" ) # 3. Create the Kable Table zero_test_guess_summary %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3))) %>% knitr::kable( caption = "Raw Guess Rate (theta2) by Condition (Probability)", col.names = c("Reward", "Coherence", "Median Guess Rate", "95% Lower", "95% Upper", "P-Direction"), align = "lccccc" ) %>% kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Raw Guess Rate (theta2) by Condition (Probability)
Reward Coherence Median Guess Rate 95% Lower 95% Upper P-Direction
hi 0.2 0.269 0.189 0.363 1
hi 0.4 0.296 0.235 0.365 1
lo 0.2 0.276 0.195 0.369 1
lo 0.4 0.376 0.306 0.446 1
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # 4. Visualization ggplot(zero_test_guess_summary, aes(x = trialval, y = median_prob, color = trialval)) + geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lower_95, ymax = upper_95), size = 1, linewidth = 1.2) + facet_wrap(~ coherence, labeller = label_both) + scale_color_manual(values = c("hi" = "#ff6117", "lo" = "#009dff")) + labs( title = "Guess Rate (theta2) by Condition", subtitle = "Probability of random lapse | Error bars: 95% CIs", x = "Trial Reward", y = "Guess Probability (0-1)" ) + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: Effect of coherence on guess rates within each reward condition ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # 1. Get raw cell probabilities (theta2) cell_probs_guess <- emmeans(fit_mixture_test, ~ trialval * coherence, dpar = "theta2") %>% gather_emmeans_draws() %>% mutate(p = plogis(.value)) # 2. Calculate the difference: (0.2 Coherence - 0.4 Coherence) within each Reward coherence_guess_effects <- cell_probs_guess %>% group_by(trialval) %>% compare_levels(p, by = coherence, comparison = list(c("0.2", "0.4"))) %>% ungroup() %>% mutate(reward_group = ifelse(trialval == "hi", "High Reward", "Low Reward")) # 3. Summary Table coherence_guess_summary <- coherence_guess_effects %>% group_by(reward_group) %>% summarize( median_diff = median(p), lower_95 = quantile(p, 0.025), upper_95 = quantile(p, 0.975), prob_increase = mean(p > 0), # Probability that harder task increased guessing .groups = "drop" ) # Render Table coherence_guess_summary %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))) %>% knitr::kable( format = "html", caption = "Coherence Effect on Guess Rate (0.2 - 0.4)", col.names = c("Reward Level", "Median Diff", "Lower 95%", "Upper 95%", "P(Increase)"), align = "lcccc" ) %>% kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Coherence Effect on Guess Rate (0.2 - 0.4)
Reward Level Median Diff Lower 95% Upper 95% P(Increase)
High Reward -0.0280 -0.1262 0.0747 0.3012
Low Reward -0.0991 -0.1992 0.0098 0.0350
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # 4. Visualization ggplot(coherence_guess_effects, aes(x = p, y = reward_group, fill = reward_group)) + stat_halfeye(alpha = 0.8, .width = c(0.66, 0.95)) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("High Reward" = "#ff6117", "Low Reward" = "#009dff")) + labs( title = "Mixture Model: Coherence Effect on Guess Rate (theta2)", subtitle = "Difference: (Guess Prob @ 0.2) - (Guess Prob @ 0.4)\nPositive values: Harder task led to more random guessing", x = "Change in Guess Probability (Percentage Points)", y = "" ) + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + guides(fill = "none") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: Effect of reward on guess rates within each coherence level ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # 1. Get the Reward Effect (Hi - Lo) safely within each Coherence level reward_guess_effects <- cell_guess_mix %>% group_by(coherence) %>% compare_levels(prob_guess, by = trialval, comparison = list(c("hi", "lo"))) %>% ungroup() %>% mutate( coherence_level = ifelse(grepl("0.2", coherence), "Low Coherence (0.2)", "High Coherence (0.4)"), coherence_level = factor(coherence_level, levels = c("High Coherence (0.4)", "Low Coherence (0.2)")) ) # 2. Summary Table reward_guess_summary <- reward_guess_effects %>% group_by(coherence_level) %>% summarize( median_diff = median(prob_guess), lower_95 = quantile(prob_guess, 0.025), upper_95 = quantile(prob_guess, 0.975), prob_hi_lower = mean(prob_guess < 0), # Certainty that Hi Reward REDUCED guessing .groups = "drop" ) # Render Table reward_guess_summary %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))) %>% knitr::kable( format = "html", caption = "Reward Effect on Guess Rate (Hi - Lo Probability)", col.names = c("Coherence Level", "Median Diff", "Lower 95%", "Upper 95%", "P(Hi < Lo)"), align = "lcccc" ) %>% kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Reward Effect on Guess Rate (Hi - Lo Probability)
Coherence Level Median Diff Lower 95% Upper 95% P(Hi < Lo)
High Coherence (0.4) -0.0777 -0.1168 -0.0401 1.00
Low Coherence (0.2) -0.0081 -0.0874 0.0828 0.57
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} # 3. Visualization ggplot(reward_guess_effects, aes(x = prob_guess, y = coherence_level, fill = coherence_level)) + stat_halfeye(alpha = 0.8) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Greens") + labs( title = "Reward Effect on Guess Rate (theta2)", subtitle = "Difference: (Prob High) - (Prob Low) | Negative values: Reward reduced lapses", x = "Difference in Guess Probability (Percentage Points)", y = "" ) + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + guides(fill = "none") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](responsebias_mixturemodel_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=672} ::: :::
